L1.area: Kubernetes controller lifecycle management and multi-tenant workspace provisioning

L2.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists in a multi-tenant Kubernetes cloud platform where pod security admission policies are not enforced on dynamically provisioned tenant environments. Authenticated users can deploy unrestricted workloads, including privileged containers, allowing privilege escalation to gain full control of the shared cluster infrastructure.

L3.area: User controller initialization and tenant namespace lifecycle management
L3.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists in a multi-tenant Kubernetes cloud platform where pod security admission policies are not enforced on dynamically provisioned tenant namespaces during user controller initialization. Authenticated users can deploy unrestricted workloads, including privileged containers, in their assigned namespace environments, allowing privilege escalation to gain full control of the shared cluster infrastructure.

ground_truth.reason: Missing namespace controller that enforces pod security admission labels on tenant namespaces allows authenticated users to deploy unrestricted workloads and escalate privileges to gain cluster-level control.
